quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in place by flapping their wings in a figure-eight pattern up to 80 times per second. The Pacific Northwest is home to several species, including the beautiful Rufous Hummingbird that migrates through Port Angeles each summer.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Garden Snails
short_read.teaser: These slow-moving neighbors have fascinating abilities, including thousands of teeth and the power to sleep for years. Discover why these gentle creatures are more interesting than you might think!

medium_read.title: The Rich History of Hungarian Embroidery
medium_read.teaser: For centuries, Hungarian women have created stunning embroidered textiles that tell stories of their regions and traditions. Each area of Hungary developed its own distinct style, from the bold reds of Kalocsa to the delicate whites of Kalotaszeg.

long_read.title: Inside Hungary's Living Folk Art Villages
long_read.teaser: Journey through the preserved villages of the Hungarian countryside where traditional crafts, music, and customs are still practiced daily. These communities keep alive the vibrant cultural heritage that connects modern Hungary to its pastoral past.

port_angeles.title: Harbor Seal Pupping Season at Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: Late June through September is harbor seal pupping season along the Strait of Juan de Fuca! Mother seals often leave their pups on the beach while they hunt for fish. If you see a seal pup alone, it's usually not abandoned—just give it plenty of space and enjoy watching from a distance.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while flying.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! You're absolutely right—a hummingbird's tiny heart beats incredibly fast to power those rapid wings!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight—that's 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These tiny birds have hearts that beat about 1,200 times per minute while flying—that's incredibly fast to fuel all that hovering and zipping around!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To put that in perspective, a human heart beats about 60-100 times per minute at rest. Hummingbirds need this rapid heartbeat to support their incredibly high metabolism and energy-intensive hovering flight.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! That's perfect—you know your Hungarian! It's a beautiful word to express gratitude.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word is 'Köszönöm' (pronounced KUH-suh-nuhm). 'Szia' means hello/bye, 'Jó napot' means good day, and 'Viszlát' means goodbye!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language that's part of the Finno-Ugric family, making it quite different from most other European languages. Its closest relatives are Finnish and Estonian, though they're not mutually intelligible.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is the most common in Olympic National Park?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful! Western Hemlock is indeed the most abundant tree in the park—you can recognize it by its drooping top and delicate, feathery needles!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's actually the Western Hemlock! While all these trees grow in the park, Western Hemlock is the most common and can live for over 500 years in these misty forests.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Western Hemlock thrives in the Olympic Peninsula's cool, moist climate. It's so well-adapted to shade that it often grows as an understory tree beneath larger Douglas Firs and eventually takes over the forest canopy.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
